title: Where the Wild Things Are
summary: A young boy named Max, dressed in a wolf suit, causes mischief and is sent to bed without supper. His room magically transforms into a wild forest, and he sails across an ocean to the land of the Wild Things. He tames the fearsome monsters, becomes their king, and leads a wild rumpus before feeling lonely and returning home to find his supper waiting for him, still hot.

characters.name: Max
characters.personality: Mischievous, imaginative, brave, bossy, but ultimately seeking love and comfort.
characters.visual_description: A young boy wearing a white wolf suit with a hood featuring wolf ears and whiskers, a long bushy tail, and claws on the hands and feet. He later wears a golden crown.

characters.name: The Wild Things
characters.personality: Fearsome but easily intimidated, wild, playful, and affectionate.
characters.visual_description: Large, monstrous creatures with a mix of animal traits: horns, scales, fur, feathers, sharp teeth, yellow eyes, and bird-like or reptilian claws. Some have human-like hair or beards.

characters.name: Dog
characters.visual_description: A small, fluffy white terrier dog.

pages.text: HARPER TROPHY $4.95 US $6.95 CDN WHERE THE WILD THINGS ARE STORY AND PICTURES BY MAURICE SENDAK
pages.summary: The cover of the book showing a sleeping wild thing and a boat.
pages.setting: A grassy shore by a river at night.
pages.key_interaction: A wild thing sleeps peacefully by the water.
pages.scene_motion: Gentle ripples on the water, stars twinkling in the night sky.

pages.summary: Copyright and ISBN information.

pages.text: Winner of the Caldecott Medal for the Most Distinguished Picture Book of the Year
pages.summary: Award recognition text.

pages.text: WHERE THE WILD THINGS ARE
pages.summary: Title text.

pages.text: WHERE THE WILD THINGS ARE STORY AND PICTURES BY MAURICE SENDAK Harper Trophy A Division of HarperCollinsPublishers Copyright © 1963 by Maurice Sendak • Printed in the United States of America • All rights reserved • First Harper Trophy edition, 1984 • 25th Anniversary Edition
pages.summary: Title page illustration showing Max dancing with two wild things.
pages.key_interaction: Max dances playfully with two wild things.

pages.text: The night Max wore his wolf suit and made mischief of one kind
pages.summary: Introduction to Max wearing his wolf suit.
Max hammering a nail into the wall to make a tent.
pages.key_interaction: Max is hammering a nail into the wall.
pages.scene_motion: A slight shadow cast on the wall from the hanging cloth.

pages.text: and another
pages.summary: Text continuing the description of Max's mischief.
Max chasing the family dog with a fork.
pages.key_interaction: Max chases the dog down the hall.
pages.scene_motion: Dust motes floating in the air near the stairs.

pages.text: his mother called him "WILD THING!" and Max said "I'LL EAT YOU UP!" so he was sent to bed without eating anything.
pages.summary: Max is scolded by his mother and sent to bed.
Max standing angrily in his bedroom.
pages.key_interaction: Max stands alone in his room, looking upset.
pages.scene_motion: Moonlight shining softly through the window.

pages.text: That very night in Max's room a forest grew
pages.summary: Text describing the beginning of the transformation.
Trees begin to grow inside Max's bedroom.
pages.setting: Max's bedroom, transforming into a forest.
pages.key_interaction: Max looks around as trees sprout from the floor.
pages.scene_motion: Leaves rustling slightly as the trees grow, moonlight glowing.

pages.text: and grew-
pages.summary: Text continuing the transformation.
The forest grows thicker, and Max smiles.
pages.setting: A dense forest that used to be Max's room.
pages.key_interaction: Max smiles as the forest overtakes his room.
pages.scene_motion: Thick foliage swaying gently in a phantom breeze.

pages.text: and grew until his ceiling hung with vines and the walls became the world all around
pages.summary: Text describing the complete transformation of the room.
Max dances in a fully grown forest under the moon.
pages.setting: A dense, magical forest at night.
pages.key_interaction: Max dances joyfully in the forest.
pages.scene_motion: Moonlight filtering through the dense canopy of the forest.

pages.text: and an ocean tumbled by with a private boat for Max and he sailed off through night and day
pages.summary: Max sails away in his private boat.
pages.setting: An ocean with a small red sailboat.
pages.key_interaction: Max steers his boat across the ocean.
pages.scene_motion: Ocean waves rolling and crashing, sail billowing in the wind.

pages.text: and in and out of weeks and almost over a year to where the wild things are.
pages.summary: Max sails past a sea monster wild thing.
pages.key_interaction: Max sails past a large sea monster.
pages.scene_motion: Water churning around the sea monster, wind blowing the sail.

pages.text: And when he came to the place where the wild things are they roared their terrible roars and gnashed their terrible teeth and rolled their terrible eyes and showed their terrible claws
pages.summary: Max arrives at the island and is greeted by roaring wild things.
pages.setting: The shore of the island of the Wild Things.
pages.key_interaction: The wild things roar at Max as he approaches in his boat.
pages.scene_motion: Waves crashing against the grassy shore.

pages.text: till Max said "BE STILL!" and tamed them with the magic trick of staring into all their yellow eyes without blinking once and they were frightened and called him the most wild thing of all
pages.summary: Max commands the wild things to be still, and they become frightened.
pages.setting: The island of the Wild Things at night.
pages.key_interaction: Max stares down the wild things, who cower in fear.
pages.scene_motion: Stars twinkling in the night sky, a crescent moon glowing.

pages.text: and made him king of all wild things. "And now," cried Max, "let the wild rumpus start!"
pages.summary: Max is crowned king and declares the start of the wild rumpus.
pages.key_interaction: Max, wearing a crown, commands the wild things.
pages.scene_motion: Leaves rustling in the background forest.

pages.summary: The wild rumpus begins with howling at the moon.
pages.setting: The forest at night under a full moon.
pages.key_interaction: Max and the wild things dance and howl at the moon.
pages.scene_motion: Moonlight illuminating the night sky, stars twinkling.

pages.summary: The wild rumpus continues with swinging from the trees.
pages.key_interaction: Max and the wild things swing from tree branches.
pages.scene_motion: Tree branches swaying under the weight of the swinging characters.

pages.summary: The wild rumpus concludes with a marching parade.
pages.key_interaction: Max rides on the back of a wild thing as they march.
pages.scene_motion: Leaves rustling as the parade marches through the foliage.

pages.text: "Now stop!" Max said and sent the wild things off to bed without their supper. And Max the king of all wild things was lonely and wanted to be where someone loved him best of all. Then all around from far away across the world he smelled good things to eat so he gave up being king of where the wild things are.
pages.summary: Max stops the rumpus, feels lonely, and decides to go home.
pages.setting: A clearing in the forest at sunset.
pages.key_interaction: Max sits alone in a tent while the wild things sleep.
pages.scene_motion: A gentle sunset glow fading in the sky.

pages.text: But the wild things cried, "Oh please don't go- we'll eat you up-we love you so!" And Max said, "No!" The wild things roared their terrible roars and gnashed their terrible teeth and rolled their terrible eyes and showed their terrible claws but Max stepped into his private boat and waved good-bye
pages.summary: Max waves goodbye to the sad wild things and sails away.
pages.key_interaction: Max waves goodbye from his boat while the wild things beg him to stay.
pages.scene_motion: Waves lapping against the shore, wind blowing the boat's sail.

pages.text: and sailed back over a year and in and out of weeks and through a day
pages.summary: Max sails back home.
pages.key_interaction: Max sits peacefully in his boat.
pages.scene_motion: Ocean waves rolling, stars twinkling in the dark night sky.

pages.text: and into the night of his very own room where he found his supper waiting for him
pages.summary: Text describing Max's return to his room.
Max is back in his room, taking off his wolf hood, and sees his supper.
pages.key_interaction: Max smiles as he returns to his normal room.
pages.scene_motion: Moonlight shining brightly through the open window.

pages.text: and it was still hot.
pages.summary: Final text stating the supper is still hot.
